goal: Turn the Obsidian markdown vault into a proper second brain that the Pi-CEO swarm can read from and write research notes into, planned end-to-end before building.

win_condition.statement: A version-controlled vault schema (folder taxonomy + frontmatter contract + naming rules) is the single source of truth for human and swarm.
win_condition.probe: SCHEMA.md exists at vault root and lists every required frontmatter key + folder.

win_condition.statement: Swarm can read a note by path and get content + parsed frontmatter + outgoing links.
win_condition.probe: Unit test reads a fixture note and asserts frontmatter and links parsed.

win_condition.statement: Swarm can search the vault (full-text + tag + frontmatter) and get ranked note paths, not the whole vault.
win_condition.probe: Search over a fixture vault returns expected paths with a capped result count.

win_condition.statement: Swarm can write a research note that lands in the correct folder, carries valid required frontmatter, and links to >=1 existing note.
win_condition.probe: Round-trip test: write -> read back -> assert folder, frontmatter, >=1 wikilink.

win_condition.statement: Swarm can surgically edit a section of an existing note without clobbering the rest of the file.
win_condition.probe: PATCH-under-heading test leaves the rest of the file byte-identical.

win_condition.statement: Every machine-authored note is provenance-stamped (author:swarm, agent, session_id, source, created).
win_condition.probe: Validator rejects a machine note missing required provenance keys.

win_condition.statement: Concurrent/repeated writes never corrupt or silently duplicate notes; the write path is idempotent and conflict-aware.
win_condition.probe: 2-writer test: two simultaneous writes of the same note yield one consistent file, no dupe.

win_condition.statement: The vault is a git repo with automated commits on swarm writes, so every machine edit is diffable and revertable.
win_condition.probe: A test write produces a git commit matching the 'swarm: <title> [<session_id>]' convention.

win_condition.statement: The swarm never writes secrets/PII into the vault; detect-secrets runs on the write path and passes.
win_condition.probe: A note containing a fake sk-ant- token is rejected and never committed.

win_condition.statement: A human opening the vault in Obsidian finds the folder tree and graph legible; agent notes are organized.
win_condition.probe: Human sign-off after opening the vault post-integration.

win_condition.statement: The vault is registered in Pi-CEO config and research outputs are routed to it.
win_condition.probe: Swarm resolves the vault path/endpoint from config, not a hardcoded path.

win_condition.statement: An operator runbook + restore procedure exists (re-index, revert a bad write, disable swarm writes, back up).
win_condition.probe: Runbook file exists and references real commands/paths for revert and kill switch.

win_condition.statement: Swarm vault writes are logged to the observability path so silent failure is detectable.
win_condition.probe: A write produces an observability record; a failed write produces a failure record.

moves.title: Audit & harvest Synthex Obsidian code
moves.deliverable: Inventory of reusable parser/client/test code under .harness/artefacts/synthex/sandbox/Synthex/lib/obsidian/* and lib/markdown/obsidian-parser.ts with a keep/adapt/discard decision per file.
moves.verify: Inventory doc lists each file and a decision.

moves.title: Decide vault location & ownership
moves.deliverable: Documented decision: vault path, git repo vs folder, human owner.
moves.verify: Decision recorded in plan/charter.

moves.title: Write the vault schema contract
moves.deliverable: SCHEMA.md defining PARA-based folder taxonomy (Projects/Areas/Resources/Archive + Research/ + _inbox/), filename rules, and the required frontmatter contract.
moves.verify: File exists and lists every required frontmatter key and folder.

moves.title: Vault read interface
moves.deliverable: Swarm-callable read returning content + parsed frontmatter + outgoing links for a path.
moves.verify: Unit test reads a fixture note and asserts frontmatter + links parsed.

moves.title: Vault search interface
moves.deliverable: Search returning ranked note paths by full-text + tag + frontmatter field, capped result count.
moves.verify: Query returns expected paths with a result-count cap.

moves.title: Provenance frontmatter standard + validator
moves.deliverable: Required machine-write frontmatter (author:swarm, agent, session_id, source, created, summary) codified in SCHEMA.md plus a validator function.
moves.verify: Validator rejects a note missing required keys; accepts a complete one.

moves.title: Stand up the bridge: Local REST API + MCP server
moves.deliverable: Obsidian Local REST API plugin + MCP server (cyanheads pattern) exposing read/search/create/PATCH, reachable by the swarm.
moves.verify: MCP read and search return live data; PATCH-under-heading leaves rest of file intact.

branches.decider: vault runs on the swarm host with Obsidian open vs headless/server vault
vault runs on the swarm host with Obsidian open vs headless/server vault

moves.title: Make the vault a git repo with auto-commit
moves.deliverable: Vault under git with commit-on-write (message convention 'swarm: <title> [<session_id>]').
moves.verify: A test write produces a git commit with the convention.

moves.title: Secret-safety gate on the write path
moves.deliverable: detect-secrets + PII check runs on every note before commit; a hit blocks the write and logs it.
moves.verify: A note containing sk-ant-... is rejected and never written.

moves.title: Idempotent, conflict-aware write path
moves.deliverable: Deterministic slug + frontmatter id; re-writing a logical note updates in place; concurrent writes serialize via lock/single-writer queue.
moves.verify: 2-writer test: two simultaneous writes of the same note yield one consistent file, no corruption, no dupe.

moves.title: Register the vault in Pi-CEO config
moves.deliverable: Config entry pointing the swarm at the vault path/MCP endpoint + the Research/ write target.
moves.verify: Swarm resolves the vault location from config, not a hardcoded path.

moves.title: Operator runbook + restore procedure
moves.deliverable: Runbook: re-index, revert a bad agent write via git, disable swarm writes, back up.
moves.verify: Runbook exists and references real commands/paths from moves 8 and 18.

moves.title: Read-context budgeting
moves.deliverable: Read/search returns bounded context (summaries + top-N notes, not full files by default).
moves.verify: Search over a 1000-note fixture returns <= configured cap.

moves.title: Swarm write tool: write_research_note
moves.deliverable: Single swarm-facing operation that applies frontmatter contract + provenance + secret gate + folder routing and writes via the bridge.
moves.verify: Round-trip test: call it -> read back -> assert correct folder, valid frontmatter, >=1 wikilink.

moves.title: Auto-linking on write
moves.deliverable: On write, the new note is linked to >=1 existing related note via search-for-similar + wikilink insertion.
moves.verify: Writing into a seeded vault produces a note containing a [[wikilink]] to an existing note.

moves.title: Wire research outputs -> vault
moves.deliverable: Deep-research / board-meeting / scan flows that emit to .harness/+Linear also write a research note to the vault via write_research_note.
moves.verify: Run a research flow -> assert a note appears in Research/ with provenance.

moves.title: Observability for vault writes
moves.deliverable: Every swarm vault write emits an event (path, agent, session, result) through observability.py.
moves.verify: A write produces an observability record; a failed write produces a failure record.

moves.title: Kill switch for swarm writes
moves.deliverable: Env/file flag (mirroring TAO_AUTONOMY_ENABLED / HARD_STOP) making the vault read-only to the swarm without affecting human access.
moves.verify: With the flag set, a write attempt is refused and logged; reads still work.

moves.title: Seed + index the existing vault
moves.deliverable: Human's current notes normalized to the schema (or a migration note documents exceptions) and the search index built over the whole vault.
moves.verify: Search returns results from pre-existing human notes, not just agent notes.

moves.title: End-to-end acceptance + human legibility review
moves.deliverable: Scripted run exercising read -> search -> write -> surgical-edit -> git-commit -> observability, plus human opens the vault in Obsidian.
moves.verify: E2e script passes all assertions AND human sign-off on legibility (wc10).

risks.risk: Concurrent writes (swarm vs human, or two agents) corrupt or duplicate notes.
risks.response: Idempotent, locked, deterministic-slug write path; git makes corruption revertable.

risks.risk: Agents leak secrets/PII into a synced vault, leaving the controlled boundary.
risks.response: Pre-write detect-secrets + PII gate blocks the write.

risks.risk: Vault reads blow the agent token budget as the vault grows.
risks.response: Bounded, summary-first ranked search instead of full-vault dump.

risks.risk: Schema drift: agents invent folders/frontmatter, vault becomes a junk drawer.
risks.response: Frontmatter validator enforced on every write + recurring legibility review.

risks.risk: Bridge fragility: Obsidian must be running; closed Obsidian = silent write failure.
risks.response: Observability surfaces failed writes; move-7 branch gives a filesystem fallback for headless.

risks.risk: A runaway agent loop pollutes the vault with hundreds of notes and can't be stopped cleanly.
risks.response: Kill switch makes vault read-only to swarm; git revert + runbook for cleanup.
